title_original: How NVIDIA Builds Open Data for AI
title_he: How NVIDIA Builds Open Data for AI
summary_he: NVIDIA מפתחת נתונים פתוחים למערכות AI, כדי לקדם את הפיתוח של מודלי AI של מודלי AI יותר אמינים ומובנים. נתונים פתוחים יוצרים דרך יעילה יותר וזולה יותר לבניית מודלי AI של מודלי AI, ומקלים על הבחנה ושיפור.
details_he: NVIDIA מפתחת נתונים פתוחים למערכות AI, כדי לקדם את הפיתוח של מודלי AI של מודלי AI יותר אמינים ומובנים. נתונים פתוחים יוצרים דרך יעילה יותר וזולה יותר לבניית מודלי AI, ומקלים על הבחנה ושיפור. NVIDIA פועלת לצמצום את הקשיים בבניית נתונים טובים, על ידי פרסום נתונים פתוחים על HuggingFace, יחד עם תכניות אימון ופרמטרי כלי כפי שהם זמינים על GitHub. NVIDIA פרסמה כבר יותר מ-2 פטה-בייט של נתוני AI-Ready לאימון, ויותר מ-180 קבצי נתונים ו-650+ מודלי AI פתוחים. NVIDIA פרסמה קבצי נתונים פתוחים בתחומים שונים, כגון רובוטיקה, מערכות אוטונומיות, AI סובוורן, ביולוגיה ופרמטרי כלי.

title_original: As Open Models Spark AI Boom, NVIDIA Jetson Brings It to Life at the Edge
description: The Cat 306 CR mini-excavator weighs just under eight tons and fits inside a standard shipping container. It’s the machine a contractor rents when the job site is tight: a utility trench near a foundation, a basement dig in a dense neighborhood. The cab is roughly the size of a phone booth. The operator sits […]
title_he: As Open Models Spark AI Boom, NVIDIA Jetson Brings It to Life at the Edge
summary_he: NVIDIA Jetson platform enables developers to build private AI assistants at the edge, leveraging open models for real-time inference and natural voice interactions.
details_he: The NVIDIA Jetson platform, built for real-time inference in industrial and robotic systems, allows developers to create private AI assistants at the edge with zero API cost and full data privacy. This is made possible by the use of open models, which unlock new possibilities for developers to build and experiment freely. The platform supports various open models, from 2 billion to 30 billion parameters, and enables users to power morning briefings, automate daily tasks, and control smart home systems in real-time.

title_original: From raw interaction to reusable knowledge: Rethinking memory for AI agents
description: It seems counterintuitive: giving AI agents more memory can make them less effective. As interaction logs accumulate, they grow large, fill with irrelevant content, and become increasingly difficult to use. More memory means that agents must search through larger volumes of past interactions to find information relevant to the current task. Without structure, these records mix […]The postFrom raw interaction to reusable knowledge: Rethinking memory for AI agentsappeared first onMicrosoft Research.
title_he: From raw interaction to reusable knowledge: Rethinking memory for AI agents
summary_he: מערכת PlugMem פותחה כדי לשפר את יכולת הזיכרון של אגנטים AI, על ידי הפיכת היסטוריית המערבעות לידע רעיוני ומיומן.
details_he: PlugMem, מערכת זיכרון חדשה, עובדת בצורה שונה ממערכות זיכרון רגילות. במקום לאחסן קטעי טקסט או ישויות שמות, PlugMem משתמשת בעובדות ובמיומנויות רעיוניות כבנייני היסוד של הזיכרון. תכונה זו מקטינה את התפלוגרפיה, מגדילה את צפיפות המידע ומשפרת את דיוק החיפוש. PlugMem נבנתה סביב שלושה יחידות עיקריות: תוכנה, חיפוש ומערכת. PlugMem יכולה לשפר את ביצועי האגנטים במגוון ניסויים, תוך שימוש בפחות יחידות זיכרון.

title_original: NVIDIA Virtualizes Game Development With RTX PRO Server
description: Game development teams are working across larger worlds, more complex pipelines and more distributed teams than ever. At the same time, many studios still rely on fixed, desk-bound GPU hardware for critical production work. At the Game Developers Conference (GDC) this week in San Francisco, NVIDIA is showcasing a new approach to bring together disparate […]
title_he: NVIDIA Virtualizes Game Development With RTX PRO Server
summary_he: NVIDIA הציגה פתרון לבעיית עבודה עם GPU בתעשיית המשחקים, על ידי הצגת רשתות GPU וירטואליות.
details_he: NVIDIA הציגה את RTX PRO Server, שמאפשרת עבודה עם GPU וירטואלית, וכך למזער את העבודה עם GPU פיזי. זאת בעזרת NVIDIA RTX PRO 6000 Blackwell Server Edition GPUs ו-NVIDIA vGPU תוכנה. רשתות GPU הוירטואליות של NVIDIA מאפשרות לאולפני משחקים למרכז ולווירטואליזצ פעילויות עיקריות, כגון יצירת תוכן, הנדסה, מחקר AI ובדיקות QA, כולן על גבי תשתיות GPU משותפות במרכזי נתונים. זאת, כדי לשמור על תגובה ואיכות גרפית גבוהה, ולשפר שימוש, גמישות, בטיחות נתונים ואחידות פעולה בין צוותים ומיקומים שונים. NVIDIA RTX PRO Server מאפשרת לאולפני משחקים לעבור מסילות גידור עבודה על-פני-עבודה לתשתיות GPU מרכזיות. זאת, כדי לאפשר לאולפני משחקים לאחד משאבים, לקצוץ בהתקני GPU, לתמוך בפיתוח, בדיקות ופעילויות AI מקביליות, ולא להגדיל את פיזור העבודה.

title_original: NVIDIA and ComfyUI Streamline Local AI Video Generation for Game Developers and Creators at GDC
description: Game developers and artists are building cinematic worlds and iconic characters — raising the bar for immersive experiences on NVIDIA RTX AI PCs.  At the Game Developers Conference (GDC) in San Francisco this week, NVIDIA announced a suite of updates that streamline AI video generation for concept development and storyboarding on RTX GPUs and the […]
title_he: NVIDIA and ComfyUI Streamline Local AI Video Generation for Game Developers and Creators at GDC
summary_he: NVIDIA and ComfyUI have collaborated to improve AI video generation for game developers and creators, introducing a simplified interface and faster 4K upscaling capabilities.
details_he: The collaboration, announced at the Game Developers Conference, includes ComfyUI's new App View with a simplified interface, lowering the barrier for entry for the popular generative AI tool. Additionally, NVIDIA RTX Video Super Resolution is now available for ComfyUI, allowing for real-time 4K upscaling. This feature is ideal for video generation and is also available as a Python Wheel for developers. Furthermore, NVFP4 and FP8 model variants are available for FLUX.2 Klein, with NVFP4 support for LTX-2.3 coming soon, delivering up to 2.5x performance gains and 60% lower memory usage for both models.

title_original: Identity governance gaps: How AI profiles move security beyond the label
description: If your identity governance program feels like a relic from a simpler time, you’re not alone. Traditional identity governance and automation (IGA) was built for a world where job titles told the whole story. A...
title_he: Identity governance gaps: How AI profiles move security beyond the label
summary_he: Identity governance programs are evolving to address the complexities of modern AI and automation, moving beyond traditional job titles and labels to incorporate AI profiles and machine identities.
details_he: The shift in identity governance is driven by the increasing use of AI and automation in various industries, including manufacturing, banking, and cloud computing. As a result, organizations are facing new challenges in managing access and trust, particularly in the context of machine identities and autonomous AI agents. The traditional approach to identity governance, which relied on job titles and labels, is no longer sufficient to address the complexities of modern AI and automation. Instead, organizations are turning to AI profiles and machine identities to move security beyond the label and ensure more effective access control and risk management.

title_original: Iranian MOIS Actors & the Cyber Crime Connection
description: Key Points Iran-linked actors are increasingly engaging with the cyber crime ecosystem. Their activity suggests a growing reliance on criminal tools, services, and operational models in support of state objectives. Iranian actors have long used cyber crime and hacktivism as cover for destructive activity, but the trend now suggests direct engagement with the criminal ecosystem. […]The postIranian MOIS Actors & the Cyber Crime Connectionappeared first onCheck Point Research.
title_he: Iranian MOIS Actors & the Cyber Crime Connection
summary_he: Iranian intelligence services are increasingly using cybercrime as an operational resource, rather than just a cover for their activities. This shift is evident in the use of ransomware, commercial infostealers, and affiliation with the cybercrime ecosystem.
details_he: The trend of Iranian actors linked to the Ministry of Intelligence and Security (MOIS) using cybercrime as an operational resource is a significant development. This approach allows them to expand their operational reach and enhance their technical capabilities. The use of ransomware branding, commercial infostealers, and affiliation with the cybercrime ecosystem is a departure from simply imitating cybercrime behavior. This shift is reminiscent of the cooperation between Iran's intelligence services and criminal actors in the physical world, where they used criminal networks for surveillance, kidnappings, and other violent acts. The U.S. Treasury, FBI, and Swedish Security Service have all documented instances of MOIS involvement with criminal networks, highlighting the regime's willingness to use any means necessary to achieve its objectives.

title_original: FortiGate Edge Intrusions | Stolen Service Accounts Lead to Rogue Workstations and Deep AD Compromise
description: FortiGate SSO flaws allows attackers to steal configs, abuse AD creds, deploy RMM tools, and exfiltrate NTDS files.
title_he: FortiGate Edge Intrusions | Stolen Service Accounts Lead to Rogue Workstations and Deep AD Compromise
summary_he: Fortinet's FortiGate Next-Generation Firewall appliances have been compromised in several incidents, allowing attackers to extract configuration files and gain access to sensitive information. The attackers then used this access to establish rogue workstations and compromise Active Directory. The compromised appliances often lacked sufficient logs, making it difficult to track the attackers' movements.
details_he: SentinelOne's Digital Forensics & Incident Response team has responded to several incidents where FortiGate appliances were compromised. The attackers exploited high-severity vulnerabilities in the appliances, including CVE-2025-59718, CVE-2025-59719, and CVE-2026-24858. These vulnerabilities allowed the attackers to extract configuration files, which contained service account credentials and network topology information. The compromised appliances often lacked sufficient logs, making it difficult to track the attackers' movements. The dwell time between initial compromise and network compromise varied significantly across the incidents, ranging from 2 months to near instantaneous follow-on activity.

title_original: Global Cyber Attacks Remain Near Record Highs in February 2026 Despite Ransomware Decline
description: Global Attack Volumes Remain Elevated Worldwide  In February 2026, global cyber attack activity remained near record levels, confirming that elevated attack volumes are becoming the new normal for organizations worldwide. The average number of weekly cyber attacks per organization reached 2,086, representing a 9.6% increase year over year, while remaining essentially flat month over month (-0.2% compared to January 2026). This stabilization at a high baseline reflects a sustained pressure environment rather than a short‑term surge. Despite a slowdown in ransomware activity compared to the same period last year, overall attack volumes continue to rise, driven by automation, expanding digital footprints, and persistent exposure risks linked to enterprise GenAI usage. Check Point Research data shows that February’s […]The postGlobal Cyber Attacks Remain Near Record Highs in February 2026 Despite Ransomware Declineappeared first onCheck Point Blog.
title_he: Global Cyber Attacks Remain Near Record Highs in February 2026 Despite Ransomware Decline
summary_he: Global cyber attack activity remained near record highs in February 2026, despite a decline in ransomware activity. The average number of weekly cyber attacks per organization reached 2,086, a 9.6% increase year over year. This sustained pressure environment reflects a continuous threat landscape.
details_he: The Check Point Research data shows that February's figures reinforce a broader trend: cyber threats are not episodic but continuous, with attackers maintaining a steady operational tempo across regions and industries. Critical sectors such as education, government, and telecommunications remained under heavy pressure, with the education sector facing an average of 4,749 cyber attacks per organization per week. Regional threat disparities persist, with emerging and rapidly digitizing regions bearing the highest burden, such as Latin America, which recorded the highest average number of attacks worldwide.

description: Kaspersky researchers identified a new Android Trojan dubbed BeatBanker targeting Brazil, posing as government apps and Google Play Store, and capable of both crypto mining and stealing banking data.
summary_he: Kaspersky researchers identified a new Android Trojan dubbed BeatBanker targeting Brazil, posing as government apps and Google Play Store, and capable of both crypto mining and stealing banking data.
